description: In Swift, use the package-local commands (swift build (or the Xcode scheme) for the right platform and configuration) rather than a root or generic command.
body: In Swift, the real contract lives in the owning package's config; a root or generic command can use the wrong the Swift toolchain version, the SDK/platform, and the build configuration or skip checks. Use when building, testing, or analyzing a specific Swift package, especially in a monorepo. Do not assume a top-level command covers the package, and avoid running a generic command that ignores its config. Actions: find the package's own targets, run swift build (or the Xcode scheme) for the right platform and configuration and swift test (XCTest/swift-testing) on the affected target scoped to it, and compare results against the package's configured gate. Validation signals include the package-scoped command log, its exit code, and a swift build -warnings-as-errors and the Xcode static analyzer pass using the package config. Risk boundary: escalate if no package-local command exists or if it depends on environment you cannot safely provision.
